column1: What is the general definition of a hackathon according to the HEC Gen AI course material?
column2: An event where teams think of an idea and build a complete application within a fixed, short duration.

column1: The specific HEC Gen AI Hackathon discussed in the session is conducted in which format?
column2: Online or virtual format.

column1: What is the total duration in hours for the HEC Gen AI Hackathon?
column2: Approximately 51 hours.

column1: On what date and time does the HEC Gen AI Hackathon officially begin?
column2: Friday, November 21st at 8:00 PM.

column1: On what date and time does the HEC Gen AI Hackathon officially end?
column2: Sunday, November 23rd at 11:00 PM.

column1: What is the required team size range for this hackathon, including the leader?
column2: Minimum of 4 and maximum of 6 members.

column1: Who is the only person responsible for submitting the hackathon registration form?
column2: The Team Leader.

column1: What is the deadline for leaders to register a new project idea during the event?
column2: Saturday at 4:00 PM.

column1: By what method should a team leader add new members or update project links after the initial registration?
column2: By using the 'Edit Response' link received in their registration confirmation email.

column1: Which four specific links must be provided in the final hackathon submission?
column2: Deployment link, code link, presentation slides link, and presentation video link.

column1: Term: MVP
column2: Definition: Minimal Viable Product, which showcases the core functionality of an application.

column1: What is the maximum allowed duration for the project presentation video?
column2: 4 to 5 minutes.

column1: In which languages can the hackathon presentation video be recorded?
column2: Urdu or English.

column1: What must be included in the presentation video to demonstrate the application's functionality?
column2: A short live demo lasting 30 to 45 seconds.

column1: The primary goal of participating in this specific hackathon is _____ rather than prize money.
column2: learning and portfolio building

column1: Why is it recommended to use online tools like Google Slides for the presentation?
column2: To easily generate a shareable public link for the submission.

column1: Which platform is suggested for finding ready-made presentation templates based on themes like healthcare or engineering?
column2: Slidesgo or Slides Carnival.

column1: Under what condition should a Hugging Face Space be set to 'Public'?
column2: To ensure the judges can access and view the working application and code.

column1: What must the presentation video include to explain the technical flow of the application?
column2: A high-level conceptual diagram showing how data moves through the system.

column1: To help with idea generation, participants are encouraged to look at the 17 United Nations _____.
column2: Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

column1: How can non-technical participants contribute to a hackathon team according to the session?
column2: By focusing on idea generation, presentation design, and project documentation.

column1: Which AI tool is explicitly mentioned as a resource for generating code, flowcharts, and presentation content?
column2: ChatGPT (GPT).

column1: What is the role of a 'Mentor' during the hackathon process?
column2: To provide guidance and search suggestions when a team is stuck, rather than doing the work for them.

column1: Who is eligible to receive a certificate upon completion of the hackathon?
column2: Only registered participants of the HEC Gen AI course.

column1: What criteria does the evaluation process use regarding 'Business Perspective'?
column2: Whether the idea can create new jobs or solve significant problems at an international level.

column1: In terms of 'Technical Evaluation', why is a simple hard-coded logic calculator insufficient for this hackathon?
column2: The project must specifically utilize Generative AI models and APIs.

column1: When deploying on Hugging Face, where should sensitive information like API keys be stored?
column2: In the 'Secrets' section of the space settings.

column1: What is the recommended tool for recording the presentation video as mentioned in the guidelines?
column2: Zoom.

column1: Participants can find potential teammates by posting their skills in the _____.
column2: WhatsApp community groups

column1: What is the first step a participant should take to find an existing project to join?
column2: Check the 'Active Projects' list for teams with empty member slots.

column1: According to the session, how does hackathon participation enhance a professional CV?
column2: It demonstrates the ability to work in teams and handle projects across different time zones.

column1: How can a team leader organize multiple files and folders for deployment on Hugging Face?
column2: By including the folder name followed by a forward slash when naming the file.

column1: What is the evaluation significance of the 'Top 10' projects?
column2: They are showcased on LinkedIn and may receive further funding or support from Aspire Pakistan.

column1: What should be the focus of the 'Live Demo' section of the presentation video?
column2: Displaying all core functionalities of the working application within a short timeframe.

column1: Which specific AI platforms were highlighted for deployment and API usage during the training?
column2: Hugging Face, Streamlit, Gemini, Claude, and Groq.

column1: Under what circumstance would the hackathon deadline be adjusted according to the speaker?
column2: If there are significant country-wide internet connectivity issues.

column1: What is the purpose of the 'Unique ID' field in the registration form?
column2: It is an optional field for the ID provided to participants via email.

column1: The 'Front-end' of the application can be built using Python libraries such as _____ or _____.
column2: Gradio; Streamlit

column1: How can a leader verify that their project updates were successfully recorded?
column2: By checking the 'Active Projects' sheet to see if the live data reflects the changes.

column1: In the context of the hackathon, what does 'AI Ideation' involve?
column2: Using GPT to research industry problems and brainstorm potential AI-driven solutions.

column1: Why is 'UI Design' considered a factor in the final evaluation of the project?
column2: It determines how appealing and user-friendly the application is to a potential user.

column1: What must teams include on the final slide of their presentation?
column2: Details and information about all the team members who worked on the project.

column1: How should a team handle a situation where their Gemini API tokens expire during judging?
column2: Mention the model used in the technical diagram so judges can potentially use their own keys to test.

column1: What is the primary benefit of using 'Version History' in tools like Google Colab during the hackathon?
column2: It allows judges to verify that the code was developed during the 48-hour event window.

column1: The 'Problem Statement' in the presentation should describe _____.
column2: the specific challenge or pain point the application aims to solve

column1: What is the recommended approach for non-registered members who wish to participate?
column2: They can join any team to learn, but they will not receive an official certificate.

column1: What is the suggested method for sharing a presentation video if it is not uploaded to YouTube?
column2: Upload it to Google Drive and ensure the sharing settings are set to 'Public'.

column1: Which organizational entities are mentioned as partners for this hackathon?
column2: Pak Angels, iCodeGuru, and ASPIRE Pakistan.

column1: What is the first action a team leader takes after submitting the registration form to enable future edits?
column2: Saves the confirmation email containing the edit link.

column1: A hackathon project that effectively integrates multiple _____ is often viewed more favorably.
column2: Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)
